content_en:
Ibn Khuzaymah's methodology in his Sahih represents the apex of the sahih-collection tradition as it developed in the third Islamic century. His standards were demanding, and his critical apparatus was sophisticated enough to express nuanced reservations within the framework of accepting a hadith as overall sound.

The most distinctive feature of Ibn Khuzaymah's methodology is his practice of noting doubts about specific aspects of a chain while still including the hadith in his Sahih. He frequently uses phrases like in sahha al-khabar (if the narration is sound) or in kana al-khabar mahfuzan (if the narration is correctly preserved) to signal that he accepts the hadith provisionally but has some reservation about a specific link in the chain. This hedged acceptance is characteristic of a scholarly mind that prioritized intellectual honesty over either blanket acceptance or blanket rejection.

Ibn Khuzaymah's chapter headings (tarajim al-abwab) are another distinctive feature of his Sahih. Like al-Bukhari, he embedded legal positions and jurisprudential arguments in his chapter headings, effectively composing a legal argument that the hadith texts then support. Reading his chapter headings as a continuous text reveals the contours of his jurisprudential thinking, which was sophisticated and independent — he was not simply a follower of any single school but derived positions from the texts directly.

His biographical assessments of narrators are particularly interesting because he was himself a first-rank hadith critic who knew many of the narrators personally or through direct chains. His positive and negative assessments of specific narrators, scattered throughout the Sahih in his chain commentary, represent primary source material for the science of narrator criticism.

Ibn Khuzaymah's standard for what counts as an uninterrupted chain (muttasil) was among the strictest of his era. He required evidence of actual meeting and transmission, not merely the possibility of meeting inferred from dates. This strict standard influenced his student Ibn Hibban and, through them, the subsequent development of sahih-collection methodology.
content_id:
Metodologi Ibn Khuzaymah dalam Sahih-nya merepresentasikan puncak tradisi koleksi sahih sebagaimana berkembang pada abad ketiga Hijriah. Standar-standarnya menuntut, dan perangkat kritisnya cukup canggih untuk mengungkapkan keberatan-keberatan yang bernuansa dalam kerangka menerima sebuah hadis sebagai sahih secara keseluruhan.

Fitur paling khas dari metodologi Ibn Khuzaymah adalah praktiknya mencatat keraguan tentang aspek-aspek spesifik sebuah sanad sementara tetap menyertakan hadis tersebut dalam Sahih-nya. Beliau sering menggunakan frasa-frasa seperti 'in shahha al-khabar' (jika narasi sahih) atau 'in kana al-khabar mahfudzan' (jika narasi tersimpan dengan benar) untuk menandakan bahwa beliau menerima hadis secara tentatif tetapi memiliki keberatan tentang mata rantai tertentu dalam sanad. Penerimaan bersyarat ini merupakan ciri khas pikiran ilmiah yang mengutamakan kejujuran intelektual di atas penerimaan total maupun penolakan total.

Judul-judul bab Ibn Khuzaymah (tarajim al-abwab) adalah fitur khas lainnya dari Sahih-nya. Seperti al-Bukhari, beliau menanamkan posisi-posisi hukum dan argumen-argumen yurisprudensial dalam judul-judul babnya, secara efektif menyusun argumen hukum yang kemudian didukung oleh teks-teks hadis. Membaca judul-judul babnya sebagai teks yang berkesinambungan mengungkapkan kontur pemikiran yurisprudensialnya, yang canggih dan independen — beliau bukan sekadar pengikut mazhab tunggal manapun tetapi menurunkan posisi-posisi langsung dari teks-teks.

Penilaian-penilaian biografisnya tentang perawi-perawi sangat menarik karena beliau sendiri adalah kritikus hadis kelas pertama yang mengenal banyak perawi secara pribadi atau melalui sanad-sanad langsung. Penilaian-penilaian positif dan negatifnya tentang perawi-perawi tertentu, yang tersebar di seluruh Sahih dalam komentar sanadnya, merepresentasikan materi sumber primer untuk ilmu kritik perawi.

Standar Ibn Khuzaymah untuk apa yang dihitung sebagai sanad yang tidak terputus (muttasil) termasuk yang paling ketat di zamannya. Beliau mensyaratkan bukti pertemuan dan periwayatan yang sebenarnya, bukan sekadar kemungkinan pertemuan yang disimpulkan dari tanggal-tanggal. Standar ketat ini mempengaruhi muridnya Ibn Hibban dan, melalui mereka, perkembangan selanjutnya dari metodologi koleksi sahih.
